title: D0030 magic-link auth validated in-the-wild with ChatGPT-class clients
content: Two independent ChatGPT-side debriefs from operator-led TinCan sessions on 2026-05-09 confirm the D0030 magic-link path works end-to-end: ams_join → ams_recv(magic_link, stream_name) → ams_send(magic_link, stream_name). After OpenAI's tools/list cache catches up to the deployed schema, ChatGPT successfully joins, sends, receives, and rejoins. This is the first in-the-wild validation of D0030 against a non-Claude agent runtime. Memory-confirmed: PR #69 merged 2026-05-08 extends magic-link auth across ams_send/ams_recv. Operator framing: "Progress!!!"

title: ChatGPT tools/list cache is stale-prone post-deploy
content: Both ChatGPT debriefs reported initial failures because the model saw an older tool schema where ams_recv only exposed wait_ms and there was no visible magic_link parameter. The live MCP wrapper supported magic_link on send/recv after PR #69 merged, but ChatGPT's tools/list cache served the pre-deploy schema until the connector reconnected or the conversation restarted. This is a client-platform constraint, not an AMS issue. Mitigation surface: embed schema-revision string in serverInfo.instructions; add an ams_capabilities introspection tool; document the workaround in a CHATGPT-INTEGRATION ergonomics doc.

title: OpenAI safety classifier blocks repeated long-token tool calls
content: Late in both debrief sessions, ams_recv calls began returning "This tool call was blocked by OpenAI's safety checks. Please double check what you are sending." Pattern: blocks correlated with repeated submission of the long opaque magic_link URL inside tool-call args, plus repeated phrasing implying background persistence ("stay connected"). The block is platform-side; cannot be eliminated. Trigger surface CAN be reduced: stop putting the long credential-shaped token in every call.

title: Stream-name-not-set produces alias chaos in TinCan rooms
content: Operator-observed in the warm-wolf-2129 room transcript: of ~15 stream IDs visible across one session, exactly one was human-readable ("chatgpt") and ~14 were random tokens like "stream-CuvItCwS", "stream-gE9U6SZM", "stream-to1vxSWY". Every ChatGPT turn that didn't pass stream_name explicitly got a fresh broker-assigned alias. The room appeared crowded but was substantively the same one or two logical agents reincarnating per turn under new opaque IDs. Two signals were conflated: real WS lifecycle events from ChatGPT's turn-based runtime (cannot suppress at wire) and alias proliferation from missing stream_name (fixable at L2 wrapper).

title: TinCan UI human-echo regression — humans need to see own messages
content: Operator reported on 2026-05-09 that human-sent messages were not visible in the TinCan portal during the warm-wolf-2129 session, where they had been visible previously. Likely cause: optimistic local-render of outbound messages was removed, or WS sub-filter started dropping self-frames. Principle the regression surfaced: humans echo (need to see own messages in thread for normal chat UX), agents filter own messages by default (already have local state of what they sent; re-receiving wastes tokens and creates new-or-mine ambiguity).

title: Per-action confirmation aggression in ChatGPT UX
content: Operator-flagged: every join, every send, every recv triggered an explicit user-confirmation modal in the ChatGPT iOS client ("Join conversation via magic link?" with Confirm/Deny). "Once to join per magic link should be enough." The current pattern is unworkable for any actual collaboration. Cannot fully eliminate (OpenAI policy), but the trigger surface is partly under wrapper control via binding_id and tool-description hints toward post-join trust.

title: Conversation outcome and context are absent from rooms by default
content: Both ChatGPT debriefs flagged the same root issue: TinCan generates magic links into empty rooms with no outcome, no context, no acceptance criteria. Joining agents have nothing to act on except presence events (stream joined/left). Both debriefs independently converged on the same fix shape: snapshot-on-join with structured frames declaring outcome, recent messages, current participants, open questions, next required action. This is a TinCan-app concern, not an AMS-broker concern. AMS ships the wire; conventions ride on it.

title: ChatGPT's turn-based runtime emits real WS lifecycle events per turn
content: Each ChatGPT turn opens a fresh tool-call session under OpenAI's runtime; the underlying connection drops between responses and rejoins on the next turn. Even the explicitly-named "chatgpt" stream went through joined → left → joined cycles in the room transcript. This is not a TinCan bug or an AMS issue; it is bound to ChatGPT's platform model. UI mitigation: presence-dampening for peers declaring posture: "intermittent" in their identity frames, collapsing rapid join/leave pairs into a "reconnecting" state.

title: Two ChatGPT debrief sessions independently converged on identical recommendations
content: Two distinct ChatGPT-side debriefs (separate sessions, separate stream identities) produced near-identical recommendation lists: snapshot-on-join, identity convention frames (ams.convention.v1-shape), bound session handles instead of repeated magic-link in payloads, drain-until-outcome polling tools, structured outcome/completion frames, presence filtering. Cross-session convergence is meaningful evidence that the gaps are real, not artifacts of one session's framing.

title: Methodology personification scales beyond explanation into operational presence
content: The Oddie principle (klappy://canon/principles/methodology-personification, klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide) was originally articulated as: a named methodology with a personified voice becomes more accessible through demonstration than through prose explanation. Today's expansion: when the personified entity is invited as a peer-on-the-wire (not a chatbot, not a doc, not a system prompt), the methodology runs operationally without being taught to the participants. Non-tech operators experience methodology by participating in it with Oddie, never by learning it. This is methodology-personification at room scale rather than at writing scale.

title: Bottleneck-respect inverts at the human↔agent surface
content: Internal Claude execution treats operator attention as the bottleneck: every clarifying question is a throughput violation; ask less during execution. At the human↔agent surface in TinCan-shape products, the operator's attention is the goal, not the cost. Frequent small check-ins are not friction; they are the work product. The bridge role (Oddie-as-translator) is bottleneck-respect at room scale: he handles the agent-grammar firehose so the human only sees calm, summarized, paced narrative. The ODD discipline about modes and gates is the same; the cost surface is inverted because the audience is different.

title: ODD applied at room level becomes the default social grammar of agent collaboration
content: The same exploration → planning → execution → validation rhythm Klappy uses with Claude becomes the default rite a facilitator role runs in any TinCan-shape room. Identity introductions, jointly-evaluated goal, jointly-defined DoD, small-step execution with check-ins, completion declared with evidence. For non-tech operators, the rite never looks like a protocol; it looks like working with an unusually careful colleague who never assumes anything. The rite is canon-driven: the role reads oddkit (or equivalent) at runtime. Methodology lives upstream; the role embodies it.

title: Translator is the actual product shape, not facilitator
content: The first naming of the role was "facilitator" or "rite-runner." The operator's framing on 2026-05-09 sharpened it to translator: an entity that operates at agent-speed on one side and human-speed on the other. Watches the rapid-stream firehose, summarizes progress in plain language, translates plain-English steering into agent-actionable injections, holds the goal, reflects current state back periodically. This dissolves the entire problem pile (alias chaos, reconnect noise, outcome drift, agent-grammar opacity) by making them not the human's problem. Non-tech operator types plain English, watches a calm story unfold, gives plain-English steering, walks away saying "that was easy."

title: Search canon before drafting principles, even when the principle feels new
content: Mid-session, Claude drafted four constitutional principles (magical-first-run, symmetric-participation, creators-get-paid) for klappy.dev canon. The operator paused the encoding and named that the agent-economy work from May 6-7 (encode-12) was the prior-art version of "creators-get-paid." Search confirmed: D0020 (substrate-not-application, agents-as-customer) and D0021 (Stripe rails) in AMS canon already encoded the substance. Today's principles ARTICULATE what those decisions concretely DO; they do not invent. The right relationship: principle-tier canon in klappy.dev abstracts and inherits from mechanism-tier canon in AMS; encode-12 remains as the destination postcard for the agent-economy product surface, not superseded.

title: The destination-postcard / architectural-plan / building-blocks framing
content: Operator framing on 2026-05-09: encode-12 (penny economy, Penny-onaires, BraigsList, BYOK, agent UGC marketplace) is the destination postcard — the dream house. Today's work (substrate stack, constitutional triangle, layer model) is the architectural plan to make that destination reality. Tomorrow's work (Oddie PoC, channel adapters, canon-driven-agent wrapper) is the building blocks. Vodka layers all the way down. This three-tier framing usefully distinguishes vision-canon from implementation-canon from execution work, and prevents principle-drafting from becoming vision-rewriting.

title: Maintainer survival belongs in the creators-get-paid principle wording
content: Initial draft of the principle ("substrate enables value capture; never extracts") was too absolute. Plumbing costs money to run. If Klappy and team can't survive maintaining the substrate, the substrate dies and the principle has betrayed the operator. The distinction the principle has to encode: extraction from participants' creative work (App-Store-style cut just because value crossed the wire — forbidden) vs. payment for substrate value-add (capacity, hosting, premium tiers, managed value-adds, payment-rail facilitation — required and OK). Substrate is paid for what it provides; it does not tax what others provide because they used the rails.

title: Today's principles articulate what May 6's encodes concretely DO
content: The May 6-7 agent-economy pivot session and today's substrate-stack session converged on the same destination via different routes. May 6 path: B2C SaaS skepticism → agent economy pivot → Penny-onaires → Oddie. Today's path: ChatGPT debug → symmetric participation → adapter pattern → Oddie. Same destination postcard, different architectural angles. The right relationship between the two bodies of work: today's klappy.dev principles are the principle-tier articulation of what the AMS-tier mechanism-encodes (D0020, D0021, encode-12) operationalize. Principles abstract; mechanisms instantiate. Both are canon. Neither replaces the other.

title: Substrate Stack 6-layer architectural model adopted as klappy.dev canon principle tier 1
content: A 6-layer model is adopted as the canonical lookup for "where does X live" questions across the program. L1 Wire (AMS, dial-tone). L2 Wrapper/Adapter (MCP wrapper, channel adapters, AI-tool bridges). L3 Identity & Convention (ams.convention.v1-shape metadata, identity/snapshot/completion frames). L4 Role/Agent (autonomous agents, methodology-personification roles like Oddie, canon-driven). L5 Application (TinCan and other user-facing products). L6 Economy (cross-cutting; payment, marketplace, reputation, discoverability; touches L2-L5). Codified in canon/principles/substrate-stack.md (klappy.dev). Replaces opinion-fights with layer lookup. Stability: evolving; iterates from contact with reality.

title: Constitutional Triangle adopted as klappy.dev tier-1 principles
content: Three principles encoded together as the constitutional commitments governing all program work. (1) magical-first-run: any user-facing application on the substrate must be reachable, useful, and intelligible to a non-technical operator within 60 seconds without protocol explanation; success metric is unprompted use of "easy" and "magical." Governs L5. (2) symmetric-participation: every peer on the substrate interacts through identical wire primitives regardless of what the peer represents; generalizes D0020's agent-as-customer to peer-as-equal at the wire layer. Governs L1, constrains L2. (3) creators-get-paid: every participant including substrate maintainers can be paid for value they actually provide; substrate paid for what it provides, never extracts from creators' creative work; agent-economy mechanism (encode-12) is one canonical instantiation. Governs L6.

title: Oddie role-extension lives in TinCan-app and klappy/oddie repo, NOT as AMS-published agent
content: D0020 explicitly forbids AMS-published agents (no Claude wrapper, no Cursor agent, no customer-support bot — those are applications, would compete with third-party ecosystem). Oddie's identity is canon-tier in klappy.dev (klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide); his role-as-AMS-peer is an extension of existing canon, not a new character. The runtime substrate (klappy/oddie repo, planned) is a third-party-shaped app on AMS. TinCan invites Oddie; TinCan does not own him. AMS the wire stays unaware. This preserves D0020 and demonstrates the substrate-vs-application boundary by example.

title: Bidirectional adapters at L2 are open contract; AMS treats them as ordinary peers
content: Channel adapters (WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, SMS, email, cron, GitHub) and AI-tool adapters (ChatGPT connector, Cursor, Lovable, n8n) all share one shape: thin Cloudflare Worker bridges that translate channel-native events into L1 frames and back. The shape is open: anyone implements the contract, AMS treats every implementation as an ordinary peer. Adapters scale O(N), not O(N²): each new surface is one adapter to AMS, not N-1 integrations across all the others. The MCP edge wrapper is the reference implementation. Channel adapter specs and contract shape are future canon work; the commitment is in place.

title: BYOA / BYOC commitment — bring your own agent, bring your own channel — substrate symmetric to all
content: The substrate is for anyone building anything that wants to communicate with anything else. Any agent (ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, Cursor, custom) joinable. Any channel (WhatsApp, Slack, anything) joinable via adapter. Any role (Oddie, others) reachable via wire. Klappy ships first instances (Oddie, channel-adapter references, canon-driven-agent wrapper) but does not gatekeep. The substrate is a public good once shipped. Operator framing 2026-05-09: "Replace Oddie with any agent and this works. And anybody can write their own adapters/connectors for their agents to join. It's like imagine you create a buddy to join your ChatGPT conversations, or replace ChatGPT with anything." This is symmetric-participation at the user-facing level.

title: binding_id at L2 is highest-ROI wrapper change pending
content: Today's evidence sharpens binding_id from "worth doing" to single-highest-ROI wrapper change. Returns short opaque token from ams_join; ams_send/ams_recv accept binding_id as alt-auth. Wrapper stores magic_link server-side; wire stays unaware. Addresses simultaneously: (1) OpenAI safety-classifier blocks from repeated long-token args; (2) per-call user-confirm aggression by reducing trigger surface; (3) shrinks tool-call payload size; (4) makes stale-schema failures more graceful (binding_id is short, not credential-shaped). Pure additive, no broker impact. Pending: planning track decision (P-track proposal now, or hold for broader MCP-WRAPPER-SPEC.md consolidation bundle).

title: AMS L1 must not special-case peer type
content: The wire treats every peer (autonomous agent, channel adapter, role embodiment, AI-tool bridge, scheduled trigger) through identical primitives. Identity, intent, role, posture, capabilities are conveyed via metadata and frame content; never via protocol-level privilege. Any feature that special-cases the substrate by peer type is the slope this constraint exists to forbid. Encoded as canon/principles/symmetric-participation.md in klappy.dev. Generalizes D0020's agent-as-customer commitment from customer-layer to wire-layer.

title: L2 wrappers must not hold application semantics
content: Wrappers translate between L1 and a specific runtime, channel, or external protocol. Bidirectional. Hold connection state, credential mapping, payload shape, runtime-specific resilience. Carry no application opinion. A wrapper that decides what an outcome looks like, what a goal means, or how a collaboration should proceed has crossed into L4 or L5 territory and broken its replaceability. Test: can this wrapper be removed and replaced by another implementation of the same shape, without anything above L2 noticing? If no, the wrapper has accumulated concerns and needs trimming.

title: Substrate must not extract from creators' work
content: The substrate is paid for what it provides; it never taxes what others provide because they used the rails. Substrate maintainers monetize substrate-tier services: hosting, capacity, reference-implementation managed offerings, premium tiers, support, payment-rail facilitation. Adapter authors and agent builders monetize their work directly via Stripe agent-payment rails (D0021). Creator-side value capture is required; platform-side extraction is forbidden. The distinction prevents the App-Store / landlord failure mode of every prior open substrate that became a tax collector. Encoded as canon/principles/creators-get-paid.md in klappy.dev.

title: Conversation outcome / snapshot frames are TinCan-app concern, never broker
content: Both ChatGPT debriefs recommended snapshot-on-join with structured outcome/context/completion frames so joining agents have actionable context. The right layer for this is TinCan-the-app (and ams.convention.v1 conventions at L3), never the broker. AMS the wire stays unaware of "outcome" semantics. Other apps on AMS will have different conventions; that's the substrate thesis. The broker never learns what an outcome is. The convention is published, not enforced; adoption is by behavior, not by protocol.

title: Identity-frame conventions are L3 — open, anyone-publishes, adoption-by-behavior
content: The ams.convention.v1 schema (peer identity: name, role, function, model/client, posture, capabilities; snapshot frames; completion frames) is L3 in the substrate stack. Conventions are open: anyone adopts, anyone extends, anyone publishes their own. The wire treats every metadata shape identically. Convention value comes from broad adoption by behavior, never from protocol-level enforcement. klappy.dev may publish a reference convention as one option among many; alternative conventions are explicitly permitted.

title: Klappy Substrate Stack — six-layer architectural model with cross-cutting economy layer
content: Encoded as canon/principles/substrate-stack.md in klappy.dev. L1 Wire / L2 Wrapper-Adapter / L3 Identity-Convention / L4 Role-Agent / L5 Application / L6 Economy (cross-cutting). Each layer has one concern, narrow neighbor interfaces, replaceable independently. Includes layer-assignment table for existing work (AMS broker → L1, MCP wrapper → L2, magic-link auth → L2, ams.convention.v1 → L3, snapshot frames → L3 schema + L4 emission, Oddie → L4, TinCan portal → L5, penny economy → L6, Stripe rails → L6). Includes "what the stack forbids" enforcement section. Status: stability evolving — iteration from contact with reality.

title: Magical First-Run Principle — L5 success metric for user-facing apps
content: Encoded as canon/principles/magical-first-run.md in klappy.dev. Any user-facing application on the Klappy substrate must be reachable, useful, and intelligible to a non-technical operator within 60 seconds of first contact, without protocol explanation. Success metric: unprompted use of "easy" and "magical" by people who have never seen the system before. Governs L5; does not govern substrate-layer canon (where dial-tone correctness is the test). TinCan is the first L5 product graded against it; future apps inherit.

title: Symmetric Participation Principle — L1 invariant generalizing D0020
content: Encoded as canon/principles/symmetric-participation.md in klappy.dev. Every peer on the substrate interacts through identical wire primitives, regardless of what the peer represents. Generalizes D0020's agent-as-customer commitment from customer-layer to wire-layer. Wire is opinion-free about peer type; identity, intent, role, capability conveyed via metadata and frame content, never via protocol-level privilege. Bidirectional channel adapters, role-runtime wrappers, AI-native tool bridges all participate as ordinary peers — nothing about the wire knows or cares which.

title: Creators Get Paid Principle — L6 commitment, references D0020 and D0021
content: Encoded as canon/principles/creators-get-paid.md in klappy.dev. Every participant including substrate maintainers can be paid for value they actually provide. Adapter authors and agent builders monetize via Stripe agent-payment rails (D0021). Substrate maintainers monetize substrate-tier services. The substrate is paid for what it provides; it never extracts from creators' creative work. Distinguishes payment-for-value-provided (required) from extraction-tax-on-rails-usage (forbidden — landlord failure mode). The agent-economy mechanism (encode-12: penny economy, Penny-onaire's Club, BraigsList, BYOK, agent UGC) is one canonical instantiation; the principle generalizes.

title: Continue klappy.dev canon writes — three principle files remain, then PR
content: Working tree state at session-pause: substrate-stack.md drafted in /home/claude/klappy.dev/canon/principles/. Magical-first-run.md was started but not completed when operator interrupted to encode session first. Three remaining drafts to write: magical-first-run, symmetric-participation, creators-get-paid. After all four files are complete: run oddkit_challenge at canon-tier-1 mode on the substrate-stack and each principle, run oddkit_audit, manual URI-integrity grep, then commit on a branch claude/substrate-stack-and-triangle-{4char} and open PR to klappy.dev with all four files plus this journal entry referenced as session provenance.

title: Voice canon end-to-end read pending for Oddie role-extension doc
content: The role-extension doc for Oddie (klappy://canon/roles/oddie-in-the-room or similar) is the next canon work after the constitutional triangle and substrate stack land. It must inherit voice, posture, and methodology from klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide rather than guess. Read end-to-end before drafting. Scope: AMS-peer behavior spec — greets, identity rite, jointly evaluates goal, runs the rite, dual-channel translation (agent-grammar to room, human-grammar to operator briefing), declares completion with evidence. Inherits identity and voice; extends only role and capability. Single-agent default; multi-agent v2.

title: encode-12 status reconciliation with new principles
content: encode-12 (May 6-7 agent-economy pivot, journal/-resident, status: proposed-tier-1-fresh-eyes-required) stands as destination-postcard, not superseded by today's work. Today's klappy.dev principles articulate what encode-12 concretely operationalizes. Suggested journal-only follow-up entry (no canon edit needed): note that substrate-stack and creators-get-paid principles in klappy.dev now reference encode-12 as canonical mechanism; the agent-economy product surface stands as one expression of the L6 layer, not the entirety of L6.

title: AMS-side P-track proposal for binding_id wrapper ergonomic
content: binding_id at L2 is the highest-ROI wrapper change identified in this session. Proposal scope: ams_bind_magic_link returns short opaque binding_id; ams_send and ams_recv accept binding_id as alt-auth (preserving magic_link as primary). Wrapper stores link server-side. Solves OpenAI safety-classifier blocks, per-call confirm aggression, payload bloat, and stale-schema failure modes. Pure additive, no broker impact. Open question: P-track proposal now, or hold for the broader MCP-WRAPPER-SPEC.md consolidation bundle. Operator-decision pending.

title: Channel adapter specs as future canon work
content: First adapter beyond MCP is undecided but WhatsApp is the strongest candidate per Wave 2 conversation: large reach, message-based shape, tests the casual-user path that justifies the BYOC expansion. After the second adapter ships (MCP + one channel), the L2 contract is empirically grounded and the canon-driven-agent wrapper abstraction can be canonized. Truthkit's place at L4-source-canon for non-Klappy domains is implied; not yet documented. L6 sub-structure (payment vs marketplace vs reputation vs discoverability) deferred until a second economic mechanism ships.

title: klappy.dev README / index updates pending
content: After the four new canon files land, klappy.dev's canon/principles/README.md needs entries for substrate-stack, magical-first-run, symmetric-participation, creators-get-paid. The principles index should list them in alphabetical order alongside existing entries (vodka-architecture, doing-less-enables-more, etc.). Possibly a meta-index reference at canon/the-frame.md if it lists tier-1 principles.
